instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same? The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon. It should consist of exactly three paragraphs, with the first paragraph introducing the idea of the book club, the second paragraph outlining its purpose and logistics, and the third paragraph inviting recipients to join. Include a bulleted list in the second paragraph with exactly three points, each containing at least 10 words and starting with the phrase "Key details include." The keyword "book club" must appear at least four times throughout the email, with one instance in the opening sentence and one in the closing sentence. The third paragraph should focus on summarizing the content without including any invitations.
response:
Subject: Join Our New Book Club

Are you ready to start a book club with friends who share your love for reading? We are excited to announce the launch of a new book club designed to bring our group together through the shared experience of literature. This initiative aims to create a welcoming space where everyone can enjoy stories and connect over their favorite authors.

Our book club will meet monthly to discuss selected books, share personal insights, and enjoy lively conversations with one another. Key details include a meeting time set for the first Saturday of every month at 7 PM. Key details include a location that will be chosen by the group to ensure comfort for all attendees. Key details include a selection of books that will be provided to members before each gathering begins. We believe this format will make participating in the book club simple and enjoyable for everyone involved.

The content of our upcoming book club sessions will focus on exploring themes, analyzing character development, and discussing the broader impact of the stories we read together. Each session is structured to encourage open dialogue while providing enough time for members to reflect on their own interpretations of the text. We look forward to seeing how our collective thoughts evolve as we navigate these literary journeys side by side.

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: The third paragraph should focus on summarizing the content without including any invitations.
Conflicts between Content Constraints.org_constraint: The third paragraph invites recipients to join.
